answer_agent:
Yes — if you join after the course has already started, you can still get a certificate, **but only if you submit your project while submissions are still being accepted**.

- You **can’t get a certificate in self-paced mode**.

- The course also requires **peer-reviewing projects**, which is only possible while the course is running and the review process is open.

So the key is: **join late is okay, as long as you’re still able to submit the project before the deadline/window closes.**

question: How do I join the Office Hours or live workshop stream if I’m a student?
answer_agent:
As a student, you don’t join the Zoom link directly.

- **Live stream:** watch on the DataTalksClub **YouTube Live** channel
- **Questions during the session:** submit them via **Slido** (the link is pinned in chat when live)
- **Announcements:** the stream link is usually posted in the **Telegram and Slack announcements channel** before it starts

Also, don’t rely on posting questions in the chat, since they may get missed.

If you want, I can also share how office hours are handled for each module.

question: What’s the best way to begin the course if I’m joining late?

1. Start with the LLM Zoomcamp docs.

   - watch the lesson videos,
   - work through the notebooks/code,
   - read the homework instructions on GitHub,
   - submit answers on the course platform before the deadline.

The videos and materials are available, so you can catch up at your own pace.

question: How do I find my own entry on the leaderboard if I can’t spot my name right away?
answer_agent:
If you can’t spot your name on the leaderboard, click the **“Jump to your record on the leaderboard”** link — your account is assigned a random display name by default, like **“Lucid Elbakyan.”**

If you want to check or change that name, go to **“Edit Course Profile”**:
- **First field:** your public display name/nickname on the leaderboard
- **Second field:** your official name for the certificate
answer_orig:
When you set up your account, you are automatically assigned a random name, such as “Lucid Elbakyan.” Click on the "Jump to your record on the leaderboard" link to find your entry.

If you want to see what your Display name is, click on the "Edit Course Profile" button.

<{IMAGE:image_1}>

- **First field:** This is your nickname/displayed name. You can change it if you want to be known by your Slack username, GitHub username, or any other nickname of your choice. This is useful if you want to remain anonymous.
- **Second field:** Change this to your official name as in your identification documents—passport, national ID card, driver's license, etc. This is mandatory if you do not want "Lucid Elbakyan" on your certificate. This name will appear on your Certificate!

question: Why do I show up with a random name on the leaderboard instead of my real one?
answer_agent:
You’re shown with a random leaderboard name because the course assigns a default anonymous display name when you create your account.

A couple of key points:

- The **leaderboard name** is separate from your **certificate name**.
- The leaderboard uses a random nickname by default, like **“Lucid Elbakyan”**.
- If you want, you can change it in **Edit Course Profile**:
  - **First field:** your public nickname/display name for the leaderboard
  - **Second field:** your official name for the certificate

So your real name won’t appear on the public leaderboard unless you set it as your display name.

question: Why does the homework content keep changing before I can submit it?
answer_agent:
If the homework title contains **`[DRAFT]`**, it means the homework is **not ready yet**, so the content can still change.

The homework is considered ready only when **both** are true:
- the homework form is open on the course management platform
- the homework title does **not** contain **`[DRAFT]`**

Until then, any work you do is at your own risk because the final version may differ.
